CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF BUTLER, JOWONNA BUTLER, BLYTH JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 7.0 DAYS
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT GARCIA, EDGAR CREAGER, JANE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS EDGAR GARCIA AND JANE CREAGER'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES (SLAPP). THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING EXCEPT AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, WHICH THE COURT TOOK UNDER SUBMISSION. ----------------------- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION AS WELL. THE DEFENDANTS SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) THE 1ST, 2ND, 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS MET ITS BURDEN UNDER THE FIRST PRONG OF THE 425.16 ANALYSIS BY SHOWING THAT THE ACTS CONSTITUTING THE GRAVAMEN OF THESE CLAIMS PRIMARILY ARISES FROM PROTECTED FREE SPEECH OR PETITIONING ACTIVITY. HAIGHT ASHBURY FREE CLINICS INC. V. HAPPENING HOUSES VENTURES, (2010) 184 CAL.APP.4TH 1539. HAVING DONE SO, THE BURDEN THEN PASSES TO PLAINTIFF ON THE SECOND PRONG OF THE ANALYSIS TO PROVE THAT IT WILL LIKELY PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT ANY ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING THAT THEY HAVE A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT STRIKES THE 1ST THROUGH 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KATY BERSAMIN, CSR #11722
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-28-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS EDGAR GARCIA AND JANE CREAGER'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES (SLAPP).

APPEARANCES: James M. Millar, Esq. of Millar & Associates 981-8100 for pltfs & opposing parties; Curtis F. Dowling, Esq. of Dowling & Marquez LLP 495-8500 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling except as to the 3rd cause of action, the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted as to the 3rd cause of action as well.
The Defendants Special Motion to Strike (Anti-SLAPP) the 1st, 2nd, 3rd and 4th causes of action is granted. Defendant has met its burden under the first prong of the 425.16 analysis by showing that the acts constituting the gravamen of these claims primarily arises from protected free speech or petitioning activity. Haight Ashbury Free Clinics Inc. v. Happening Houses Ventures, (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1539. Having done so, the burden then passes to Plaintiff on the second prong of the analysis to prove that it will likely prevail on the merits. Plaintiff fails to present any admissible evidence demonstrating that they have a probability of prevailing. Accordingly, the Court strikes the 1st through 4th causes of action. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR #11722
